title: Minimal-autocomplete appearance on long pick-lists, threshold by form purpose
description: OC4 documentation recommends appearance='minimal autocomplete' to filter dropdown options as the user types. Two thresholds apply by form purpose. PARTICIPATE/EPRO forms: trigger at 5+ choices (screen-space pressure on mobile, per OC internal best-practice doc). SITE-STAFF forms: trigger at 20+ choices (performance optimization for long lists, per OC4 public docs). Below the trigger, default appearance (vertical radio buttons / checkboxes) is fine. The skill applies the threshold per form's is_epro / Participate flag. Each list crossing its threshold gets appearance='minimal autocomplete' unless the customer library explicitly specifies otherwise.
rationale: Long dropdown lists on mobile devices are particularly painful — the user has to scroll through dozens of options to find theirs. Autocomplete-filtering reduces a 50-item list to whatever matches the user's first few keystrokes. The threshold differs by form purpose because mobile/ePRO forms have stricter screen-space constraints than site-staff forms on desktop monitors. Below the threshold, native radio/checkbox layout is more discoverable and lets the user see all options at once.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} (${field.type}) on form ${form.form_id} — check choice-list size against the form's purpose. Participate/ePRO: 5+ choices → appearance='minimal autocomplete'. Site-staff: 20+ choices → appearance='minimal autocomplete'. Below threshold: default vertical layout.

effect.soft: Count the choice list referenced by this field. If the form is Participate/ePRO (form.is_epro=true OR form purpose marked Participate) and the list has 5+ choices, set appearance='minimal autocomplete'. If the form is site-staff and the list has 20+ choices, same. Customer-library overrides preserved. Surface counts in conventions_applied.autocomplete_appearance. Choice-list counting from a field-target context isn't a current DSL primitive (would need to resolve field.choice_list_name → choices.length); handled in soft.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §10 during Phase B.1d. First convention in the new `appearance.*` top-level category (rules about presentation/UX, distinct from `field_metadata.*` which is bind:: metadata and `field_types.*` which is type semantics). Hybrid because the threshold check requires resolving choice-list size from a field-level convention — a join the engine doesn't currently do cleanly. A future DSL primitive 'field.choice_list_size' (returns length of the choices for this field's list_name) would make this structured.
